title: Segmentation Fault when non-root
body:
**Description**

<!--
Qi Wang found this one while learning about Buildah. She tried to follow the installation instructions (https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/blob/master/install.md).  She followed them as a non-root user using `sudo -s` and when she did a buildah run command received a segmentation fault.   Further investigation discovered a selinux issues, 'setenforce 0` allowed the commands to run.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Using a non-root account, 'sudo -s' and then install Buildah, either through dnf or the install instructions on GitHub.
2. `ctr=$(buildah from alpine)
3.  `buildah run $ctr /bin/sh`


**Describe the results you received:**

segmentation fault.  Journalctl showed:

```

Jul 03 10:46:15 qiwan.usersys.redhat.com dbus-daemon[1914]: avc:  received setenforce notice (enforcing=0)
Jul 03 10:46:25 qiwan.usersys.redhat.com kernel: SELinux: mount invalid.  Same superblock, different security settings for (dev mqueue, type mqueue)
Jul 03 10:46:25 qiwan.usersys.redhat.com audit[11271]: AVC avc:  denied  { read write } for  pid=11271 comm="sh" path="/dev/pts/0" dev="devpts" ino=3 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c656,c750 tcontext=system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c656,c750 tclass=chr_file permissive=1
Jul 03 10:46:25 qiwan.usersys.redhat.com audit[11271]: AVC avc:  denied  { map } for  pid=11271 comm="sh" path="/bin/busybox" dev="dm-0" ino=274287 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c656,c750 tcontext=unconfined_u:object_r:var_lib_t:s0 tclass=file permissive=1
Jul 03 10:46:25 qiwan.usersys.redhat.com audit[11271]: AVC avc:  denied  { read execute } for  pid=11271 comm="sh" path="/bin/busybox" dev="dm-0" ino=274287 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c656,c750 tcontext=unconfined_u:object_r:var_lib_t:s0 tclass=file permissive=1
Jul 03 10:46:25 qiwan.usersys.redhat.com audit[11271]: AVC avc:  denied  { ioctl } for  pid=11271 comm="sh" path="/dev/pts/0" dev="devpts" ino=3 ioctlcmd=0x5413 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c656,c750 tcontext=system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c656,c750 tclass=chr_file permissive=1
Jul 03 10:46:25 qiwan.usersys.redhat.com audit[11271]: AVC avc:  denied  { open } for  pid=11271 comm="sh" path="/dev/tty" dev="tmpfs" ino=78762 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c656,c750 tcontext=system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c656,c750 tclass=chr_file permissive=1
Jul 03 10:46:25 qiwan.usersys.redhat.com audit[11271]: AVC avc:  denied  { open } for  pid=11271 comm="sh" path="/etc/passwd" dev="dm-0" ino=274400 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c656,c750 tcontext=unconfined_u:object_r:var_lib_t:s0 tclass=file permissive=1
(END)
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

No segmentation fault.


**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
v1.2-dev
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
v1.2-dev
```

comments.body: I don't see this any longer.  I am going to close this issue, reopen if you can get it to happen again.
